chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitution Petition No. D- 661 of 2026 (Ali Raza Unar vs. Province of Sindh and others) ******* Before:- Mr. Justice Amjad Ali Bohio, Mr. Justice Ali Haider ‘Ada’. 07.05.2026. Mr. Naeemuddin Z. Kasmi, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Ali Raza Balouch, Additional Advocate General, along with Barkat Hussain Panhwar, Office Superintendent, on behalf of the Commissioner Sukkur Division, and Majid Ali Mako, Additional Deputy Commissioner–II Sukkur, on behalf of the Deputy Commissioner Sukkur, are present ----------------------- Ali Haider ‘Ada’, J:- Through this Constitutional petition, the petitioner has sought directions against the respondents on the ground that walk-in interviews were conducted for consideration of appointment against suitable posts in BPS-01 to BPS-04, in accordance with the prescribed policy. It is the case of the petitioner that he duly applied for consideration; however, Respondent No.4, the Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur, failed to consider his candidature for appointment against any of the said posts. Learned counsel for the petitioner has reiterated that despite the availability of posts and initiation of the process, only select candidates were accommodated, whereas the process was not carried out strictly in accordance with merit or through a transparent competitive mechanism. He has, therefore, prayed for appropriate directions to safeguard the petitioner’s rights under the applicable policy framework. On the other hand, Respondent No.3, Commissioner Sukkur Division, has filed a compliance report stating that the memo of the petition has already been forwarded to Respondent No.4/Deputy

He has, therefore, prayed for appropriate directions to safeguard the petitioner’s rights under the applicable policy framework. On the other hand, Respondent No.3, Commissioner Sukkur Division, has filed a compliance report stating that the memo of the petition has already been forwarded to Respondent No.4/Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur, who is also the Chairman of the District Recruitment Committee, for submission of comments and for examination of the petitioner’s case on merit. A copy of the letter dated 28.04.2026 has also been placed on record in support thereof. Furthermore, on behalf of Respondent No.4, comments have been submitted wherein it is stated that at present no advertisement or publication exists inviting applications for posts in BPS-01 to BPS- 04. However, the Additional Deputy Commissioner-II, appearing on behalf of the Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur, has submitted that as and when posts are advertised and vacancies are available, the case of the petitioner shall be considered strictly in accordance with the applicable policy and merit criteria. In view of the above, learned counsel for the petitioner, in light of the statement made by the respondents, is satisfied and has prayed for issuance of appropriate directions to ensure consideration of the petitioner’s case in recruitment process. Accordingly, Respondent No.4, / Deputy Commissioner Sukkur being the Chairman of the District Recruitment Committee, is directed to place the case of the petitioner before the District Recruitment Committee for consideration as and when posts in BPS- 01 to BPS-04 are advertised, and to ensure that the petitioner’s case is considered strictly in accordance with the prescribed policy, rules, and merit, without any discrimination. With the above observation and direction, the instant constitutional petition stands disposed of accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE
